page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an arm64 architecture powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 4.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate mixed performance characteristics. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average range. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows strong performance at 14.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, are weak. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks and large block writes. Passmark CPU, encryption, and extended instruction tests register poor results.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.large offers a low-resource entry point into the ARMv9-based Graviton family, trading off multi-threaded processing power and memory capacity for a lower cost profile. It is suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight database operations, basic text processing, and network-optimized workloads that do not require high memory density or parallelized multi-core execution.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz, 4.0 GB of system memory, and a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU. Single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng, show average performance, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, memory write bandwidth, and Passmark CPU scores are weak to poor. This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-based workloads, trading off multi-threaded performance and memory capacity. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, basic text processing, lightweight database operations, and network-optimized tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 6 Gbps network baseline, offering average single-core but weak multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An entry-level, network-optimized ARMv9 instance delivering steady single-core performance for lightweight, compute-focused workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Weak multi-core, memory write, and cryptographic benchmark results
Suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and light database operations
